text_blocks.text: A study in England showed a 0.3% prevalence of sexual
abuse by any professional, a group that included priests,
religious leaders, and case workers as well as teachers. It is
important to note, however, that the British study referenced
above is the only one of its kind and consisted of "a random
... probability sample of 2,869 young people between the ages
of 18 and 24 in a computer-assisted study" and that the
questions referred to "sexual abuse with a professional," not
necessarily a teacher. It is therefore logical to conclude
that information on the percentage of abuses by teachers in
the United Kingdom is not explicitly available and therefore
not necessarily reliable. The AAUW study, however, posed
questions about fourteen types of sexual harassment and
various degrees of frequency and included only abuses by
teachers. "The sample was drawn from a list of 80,000
schools to create a stratified two-stage sample design of
2,065 8th to 11th grade students"Its reliability was gauged
at 95% with a 4% margin of error.
